introLines.text: Vern, I have documentation, Vern. Radar logs. Witness statements. This is real.
I was told never to speak of this. But after thirty years, I can't stay silent anymore.
What I'm about to tell you could change everything we think we know.

detailLines.text: Radar contact at impossible altitudes, performing maneuvers that defy physics. Multiple witnesses, all credentialed.
We tracked it for nearly twenty minutes before it accelerated beyond our ability to follow. Command ordered silence.
The craft was unlike anything in any database. Smooth, metallic, no visible propulsion. Just... hovering.

defenseLines.text: They can discredit me all they want. I have documentation. I have witnesses. This happened.
I've got nothing to gain from this and everything to lose. Why would I fabricate this?
You think I want to be called crazy? I'm risking everything by coming forward.

acceptanceLines.text: That's exactly right. They don't want us talking about this.
Finally, someone who understands what's at stake here.
You're one of the few who gets it, Vern.

extraDetailLines.text: There's more. After the incident, we were debriefed by people I'd never seen before. No insignias, no names.
I've connected with others who've had similar experiences. The patterns are too consistent to ignore.
The technology we witnessed... it's decades beyond anything publicly acknowledged.

extraDefenseLines.text: I've set up contingencies. If something happens to me, the information goes to multiple news outlets.
My record is public. My credentials are verifiable. I have no history of fabrication.
The pressure to stay silent only confirms how important this information is.

conclusionLines.text: Keep doing what you're doing, Vern. The truth needs to get out.
I've said my piece. Do with it what you will.
